items.title: Oil Jumps as US and Iran Trade Strikes, Dispute Hormuz Status
items.author: Kanoko Matsuyama, Rong Wei Neo
items.content_excerpt: Oil jumped as the US and Iran exchanged fresh strikes, with the sides offering conflicting statements on whether the Strait of Hormuz remains open to shipping.

items.title: US Says It Began Launching More Strikes Against Iran at 5pm ET
items.author: Susanne Barton
items.content_excerpt: US Central Command forces began launching more strikes against Iran at 5 p.m. ET today, to continue degrading their ability to attack civilian mariners and commercial ships freely transiting the Strait of Hormuz, according to a post on X.

items.title: 【今朝の5本】仕事を始める前に読んでおきたい厳選ニュース
items.content_excerpt: ​米イラン応酬激化、AI開発競争、グラム上院議員死去、アップルが提訴、半導体株に慎重

items.title: Traders Grapple With World That’s Good for Dollar, Bad for Bonds
items.author: Carter Johnson, Michael MacKenzie
items.content_excerpt: What’s good for the US dollar isn’t always good for US bonds — but investors are finding ways to work around it.

items.title: NZ Aims to Sign LNG Import Plant Deal Before November Election
items.author: James Mayger
items.content_excerpt: New Zealand plans to sign a contract for its first liquefied natural gas import facility before the November election, with the government betting cheaper global LNG prices in coming years will make the fuel an affordable and reliable backup for renewable electricity.

items.title: Bloomberg This Weekend | Sen. Lindsey Graham Dead, CENTCOM Launches Strikes on Iran
items.content_excerpt: The news doesn’t stop when markets close. Hosts David Gura, Christina Ruffini and Lisa Mateo bring clarity, context and a bit of humor to the weekend’s biggest headlines, LIVE from New York. Joined by Adm. Daryl Caudle, US Dept of War Chief of Naval Operations, Kurt Volker, fmr. US Ambassador to NATO, Rep. Greg Stanton, (D) Arizona, Shannon Kingston, ABC New State Department Reporter, Romina Ruiz-Goiriena, USA Today Executive Editor Investigations, Storytelling, Caleb Silver, Investopedia Editor

items.title: Stanton Warns Hormuz Closure Hurts Economy
items.content_excerpt: Rep. Greg Stanton says the Trump administration needs a stronger diplomatic strategy to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and negotiate a more accountable agreement with Iran, warning the conflict has increased economic and geopolitical risks. He also defends closer ties with Canada, Mexico and other US allies as essential to competing with China, while condemning settler violence in the West Bank following an incident involving Rep. Ro Khanna. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Demographic Shift Tests AI Promise
items.content_excerpt: Martha Gimbel, executive director and cofounder of the Budget Lab at Yale University, says artificial intelligence is unlikely to offset the economic challenges of an aging population, arguing the US is confronting two major transitions at the same time rather than one solving the other. She says AI could improve productivity in some areas, particularly by helping caregivers with physically demanding tasks, but questions whether consumers will embrace automation in roles where human interaction

items.title: Volker Sees NATO Summit Strengthening Alliance
items.content_excerpt: Former US ambassador to NATO Kurt Volker says the NATO summit reinforces alliance unity, strengthens support for Ukraine and sends a tougher message to Russian President Vladimir Putin, while praising President Donald Trump's more constructive engagement with President Volodymyr Zelensky. Volker says Ukraine's battlefield innovation and sustained pressure on Russia are shifting the conflict's momentum, though he warns Putin remains committed to the war and is unlikely to negotiate without greate

items.title: Oil Market Prices In Hormuz Resilience
items.content_excerpt: Bloomberg Intelligence senior commodities strategist Mike McGlone says oil markets are largely accepting US assurances that the Strait of Hormuz remains open, with expanding Western Hemisphere supply and alternative export routes limiting the long-term impact of Gulf disruptions on crude prices. He says gasoline and diesel prices remain elevated because refining margins have widened, but expects crude oil prices to trend lower over time as global supply grows and demand softens. (Source: Bloombe

items.title: Navy Chief Backs Bigger NATO Role
items.content_excerpt: Chief of Naval Operations Adm. Daryl Caudle says NATO's naval alliances remain strong as the US shifts more responsibility to allies while focusing on capabilities only the US Navy can provide. He says the Navy is accelerating autonomous systems, preparing for growing competition in the Arctic and protecting global sea lanes as critical priorities for US national security. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: 情暖桑榆，健全乡村养老服务网络（深阅读·稳步推进基本公共服务均等化）
items.content_excerpt: “十五五”规划纲要提出，“健全县乡村三级养老服务网络”。山东省泰安市泰山区探索农村互助式养老模式，湖南省汨罗市将养老服务点建到农村老年人家门口，甘肃省永昌县引入专业团队把暖心服务送上门……多地深入推进养老基本公共服务均等化，持续健全县乡村三级养老服务网络，让更多农村老年人老有所养、老有所依、老有所乐、老有所安。 发挥银龄力量，探索互助养老 中午11点，泰安市泰山区邱家店镇东燕家庄村，86岁的独居老人张孝美仔细听着院门外的动静。不一会儿，脚步声近了，接着传来熟悉的声音：“婶子，我赶集回来了，这是给您捎的青菜和面条。”63岁的村民张传凤走了进来。 张奶奶的儿女常年在外，自己年事已高，行动不便，难以独自前往集市采购生活物资，张传凤便主动当起“代购员”。每到赶集日，她都会提前询问老人需求，帮忙选购日常食材和生活用品，再送到老人家中。不仅如此，张传凤还化身“跑腿员”，专程帮老人到村委会办理自来水费充值业务等。 在泰山区，像这种“年轻老人”帮衬高龄老人的事情已是寻常。为解决养老服务资源分散、高龄独居老人照料难等问题，泰山区发挥农村血缘、地缘、亲缘关系和邻里相助的优势，探索实施农村互助养老模式，吸纳
